_description: Cultural expectation profiles for hospitality guests. Applied on top of archetype baselines. Shapes complaint style, service expectations, platform preference for reviews, and the narrative tone the LLM produces.

_calibration.method: Sensation baseline modifiers derived from Hofstede 6-D cultural dimensions (2026-04-18).

mapping_rules.UAI_high_to_cleanliness_and_speed: Countries with UAI > 70 (JP, DE, ES, FR, IT, LATAM, GCC) have -5 to -10 modifiers on cleanliness/speed (they demand more structure and standards)
mapping_rules.IDV_high_to_personalization: Countries with IDV > 75 (UK, US, IT, AU, NL) have -5 to -10 on personalization (they expect individual recognition)
mapping_rules.PDI_high_to_service_deference: Countries with PDI > 60 (FR, ES, LATAM, GCC) have 0 to +3 on service_quality (accept service hierarchy)
mapping_rules.MAS_low_to_wellness_emphasis: Countries with MAS < 30 (NL, SE, NO, DK) have +2 to +5 on authenticity/aesthetic (emphasize quality of life over achievement)
mapping_rules.IVR_low_to_luxury_restraint: Countries with IVR < 40 (DE, IT, CN) are more restrained in indulgence expectations

_calibration.limitations: Hofstede dimensions are 20-30 year-old country averages; actual hotel guest behavior shows within-country variation up to 50pp. Use as DIRECTIONAL anchoring, not absolute.

german_dach.label: Germany / Austria / Switzerland
key_expectations: punctuality of all services (check-in, restaurant, spa)
rule clarity (what's included, what isn't, printed)
efficient processes without small talk delay

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=64 → cleanliness -7, speed -4. IVR=40 → culinary +2 (more restrained, less gastronomic expectations). IDV=63 neutral on personalization. MAS=72 → amenity_usability -4 (performance expectation).

signature_phrases_examples: Der Check-in dauerte 25 Minuten, was zu lang ist
review_voice.voice_guidance: Write directly. State facts with evidence (time, amount, location). Separate positives and negatives clearly. No superlatives. Criticism is factual, not emotional.

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=35 (low) → tolerant of chaos, no cleanliness/speed penalties. IDV=80 → personalization -4 (expect individual recognition). IVR=69 (high) → value -6 (sensitive to price/value ratio, pub-culture scrutiny). MAS=67 → culinary -2 (expect substantial breakfast).

signature_phrases_examples: not quite what we were led to expect
review_voice.voice_guidance: Use British understatement. 'A little disappointing' = terrible. 'Rather good' = outstanding. Criticism is indirect, often wrapped in light irony or a self-deprecating aside. Avoid American-style gushing. Dry humour is welcome.

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: IDV=86 (highest globally) → personalization -7 (require recognition). UAI=47 → modest cleanliness/speed expectations. IVR=68 → indulgent, expect amenity abundance, comfort -4. MAS=57 → service_quality -3 (performance expectation).

signature_phrases_examples: This place is a HIDDEN GEM
review_voice.voice_guidance: Open warm and effusive. Use superlatives freely (amazing, incredible, phenomenal). Name specific staff by name. All-caps for emphasis is acceptable. Multiple exclamation marks common. Explicit recommendation and return intent. On negatives, be direct but frame as 'what could be improved'.

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=88 (very high) → cleanliness -3 (but tolerate chaos differently than Germans). PDI=57 → service_quality +3 (accept hierarchy). Authenticity -5 and culinary -4 = domestic/Italian scrutinize local quality. Crowd +5 and speed +3 = social culture, accept liveliness.

signature_phrases_examples: Nos hemos sentido como en casa
El trato del personal fue exquisito
review_voice.voice_guidance: Write warmly, in first-person-plural when in couple. Emphasize human interactions — named staff, the warmth of service, the 'trato'. Food and hospitality as cultural values. Complaints are emotionally framed ('una pena') rather than factual. End with a 'repetiremos' or 'recomendable' verdict.

french.label: France
key_expectations: design aesthetic and quality of materials
respect for the art de vivre

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=86 → culinary standards highest globally, culinary -10 (anchor: France is the benchmark culture). MAS=43 (low) → aesthetic/authenticity matter (quality of life). PDI=68 → service_quality discretion-focused. Breakfast expectations high.

signature_phrases_examples: Quelques déceptions sur le plan gastronomique
Le service, bien que présent, manquait de
À ce prix-là, on pouvait s'attendre à
review_voice.voice_guidance: Write in measured, articulate French. Expect gastronomy to be judged harshly — France is the benchmark. Criticism often uses sarcastic understatement ('À ce prix-là, on pouvait s'attendre à mieux'). Reference 'art de vivre' and discretion. Superlatives feel gauche; restrained compliments are higher praise.

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: MAS=14 (very low, quality-of-life focus) → aesthetic +2 (authentic materials). PDI=28 (lowest globally after Austria) → egalitarian service expected, authenticity +2. UAI=40 → moderate cleanliness. Modernity -5 (high expectations for digital self-service). Crowd -5 (value quiet/space).

review_voice.voice_guidance: Write calmly and egalitarian. No servility to the property and no superiority either. Often written in English even by native Swedish/Danish/Finnish. Mention sustainability, design, quiet. Avoid superlatives. Praise is understated; criticism is constructive. Digital self-service and practical logistics matter.

east_asian.label: Japan / South Korea / Taiwan
key_expectations: printed information in their language ideal

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=82 + Japanese omotenashi tradition → cleanliness -10 (highest standard globally). IDV=27 (collectivist) → personalization +3 (accept group treatment, no solo recognition need). MAS=60 → service_quality -5 (service as performance art). LTO=94 (highest globally) → longer-term brand loyalty if earned.

signature_phrases_examples: 清潔感があり快適でした
スタッフの対応は丁寧でした
改善していただければ
Thank you for the nice stay
review_voice.voice_guidance: Write briefly and politely. Avoid direct criticism — what was wrong is OMITTED rather than said, or softened with 'a little' / '少し'. Emphasize cleanliness, quiet, precise service. Short reviews (150-400 characters). If something was bad, the guest quietly never returns rather than writing a bad review. When writing in English, still brief and polite.

chinese_mainland.label: Chinese mainland
key_expectations: hot water kettle and instant noodle-friendly

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: PDI=80 → service hierarchy expected. IDV=20 (very collectivist) → group facilities matter. UAI=40 (moderate) → tolerant of chaos. IVR=24 (restrained) → modernity -5 expectations specifically around tech (digital payments). MAS=66 → aesthetic -3 (status signaling). Culinary +3 only because less Chinese-specific expectation at overseas hotels.

review_voice.voice_guidance: Write for social media. Lead with photogenic / 'Instagrammable' moments. Emojis are natural. Mention 'photo-worthiness' and whether the stay is worth 打卡 (check-in). Group/family friendliness valued. Complaints are direct but surface-level (service attitude, WeChat Pay availability, water kettle presence).

middle_east_gcc.label: GCC (UAE, Saudi, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman)
key_expectations: prayer mat, qibla direction, Ramadan consideration
large rooms / connecting suites for families
luxury in materials and generosity of service

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: PDI=80 (highest cluster) → service hierarchy essential, amenity_usability -5 (generosity expected). IDV=38 → personalization -5 (family/group recognition). UAI=68 → safety -3, culinary -3 (halal/religious accommodation). Larger-suite expectation → comfort_physical -5.

review_voice.voice_guidance: Write in confident, luxury-anchored tone. Generosity of service and room size matter — call out specifically. Mention halal food, prayer facilities, family accommodation. Complaints implicitly expect management-level resolution. Often reviewed in both Arabic and English.

latin_american.label: Latin America (Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Colombia, Chile, etc.)
key_expectations: reasonable prices vs US competitor pricing

sensation_baseline_modifiers._calibration_rationale: UAI=83 → cancellation visibility matters. IDV=26 (collectivist) → group/family facilities. PDI=66 → service hierarchy accepted. IVR=73 (indulgent) → culinary +2 flexibility, crowd +3 (social acceptance). Value -3 (cost-conscious vs US but aspirational).

review_voice.voice_guidance: Write warmly in Spanish or Portuguese, first-person-plural for couples/families. Emphasize 'trato' / 'atención' / 'simpatía' — the human warmth of service. Name staff. Mention value relative to US pricing. Complaints framed warmly as 'lo único a mejorar'. Strong explicit recommendation.
